title: 3 Ways to Earn: Job, Business, Ownership
learningObjectives: Understand the three income types: earned, business, passive
See why business and ownership income scale beyond trading time
Identify opportunities to create income, not just receive it
keyPrinciples: Earned income: trade your time for money (limited by hours)
Business income: build a system that makes money
Ownership income: assets earn for you while you sleep
storyPrompts: {name} mows lawns for $15/hour. Their neighbor owns the mowing company and pays five kids to mow — keeping $5 from each job. 'Same lawn,' {name} thinks, 'totally different result.' That's the difference between a job and a business.
Three paths in front of {name}: A part-time job at the mall. A small reselling side hustle. A fractional share of a local business. Each earns differently. Which one scales beyond the hours in a day?

'{item} just earned me {amount} while I was sleeping,' {name}'s older sibling says, showing their phone. 'My savings account interest.' It's small — but it's proof the system works. Money making money.

decisionCards.scenario: You have 10 free hours this week. How do you monetize them?
decisionCards.context: An owner asks: is this trading time, or building something?

choices.label: Start a small reselling operation
choices.description: Buy low, sell high. Your first taste of business.

choices.label: Create a digital product to sell passively
choices.description: Make it once, sell it forever.

decisionCards.scenario: You have $50 to put to work. What type of income does it create?
decisionCards.context: Money can work for you — but HOW it works matters.

choices.label: Buy supplies to flip at a profit
choices.description: Business income: your effort × smart buying.

choices.label: Use it for a skill course

decisionCards.scenario: Someone offers to pay you {amount} to do their homework.
decisionCards.context: Easy money — but is it ethical? And is it sustainable?

choices.label: Offer tutoring instead
choices.description: Same skill, ethical application, repeat business.

choices.label: Decline and focus on your own work
choices.description: Your reputation is worth more than {amount}.

decisionCards.scenario: You could spend 20 hours earning $10/hour, or 5 hours building something worth $200.
decisionCards.context: This is the leverage question every entrepreneur faces.

choices.label: Build the $200 thing — if it works
Do both: part-time job + side project

decisionCards.scenario: Your small business idea could make money — but requires {amount} upfront.
decisionCards.context: Every business starts with a first investment.

choices.label: Start smaller with what you have
Build the plan first, then fund
choices.description: The plan reveals if it's worth it.

decisionCards.scenario: Your first business attempt failed. You lost {amount}.
decisionCards.context: Every successful entrepreneur has a failure story.

choices.label: Start again with the lesson applied
choices.description: Most successful businesses were born from failed ones.

decisionCards.scenario: A classmate asks you to partner on their business idea.
decisionCards.context: Partnerships can multiply OR divide — choose carefully.

choices.label: Jump in — partnerships are great!
choices.description: Two brains are better than one!

choices.label: Ask to see their plan first
choices.description: Business partners are like co-pilots. Vet them.

choices.label: Decline and work on your own idea
choices.description: Your idea has been growing too.

decisionCards.scenario: You made {amount} from your side business this month.
decisionCards.context: Your first taste of business income. Now what?

choices.label: Celebrate and spend it all
choices.description: You earned it differently — spend it differently!

choices.label: Reinvest 50% back into the business
choices.description: Compound the business, not just the money.

choices.label: Bucket split it like income
choices.description: Treat business income like any other income.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: A real business opportunity appears — but requires committing your entire savings.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Big opportunity, big risk. The Owner's ultimate test.

choices.label: Go all in — fortune favors the bold!
Invest only what you can afford to lose
Study it more before committing
choices.description: Due diligence is the owner's armor.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: Your side income is growing. But it's eating into study/sleep time.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Sustainability is part of the owner's equation.

choices.label: Systematize it so it runs without you

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: Someone offers to buy your small business for $500.

choices.label: Counter-offer for more
choices.description: The first offer is rarely the best.

choices.label: Keep it — it's just getting started
choices.description: Not every business should be sold.

forecasts: Your Courage trait is growing. Bigger business opportunities will appear next week!
Low ownership activity. Next week brings a chance to start something small — take it.
Business income unlocked! Compounding returns begin next session.
You've been playing it safe. Next week's boss will test your risk tolerance directly.
